content.plain_english: This week in EYFS, your child is exploring Find my pattern. They will focus on: sharing and grouping; even and odd; spatial reasoning 3. All of this happens through play, stories, and practical activities that build early mathematical curiosity and confidence.
content.in_school: In sessions, children explore Find my pattern through play, stories, and practical activities using real objects. Their teacher guides them through careful conversation, helping them notice patterns and relationships without formal recording.
content.home_activity: Spend a few minutes exploring find my pattern together at home. Use whatever is around — pasta shapes to sort, buttons to count, or water to pour into different containers. Ask your child to show you what they have been doing in school. There are no wrong answers — just curious conversations and playful exploration.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me something at home that is about find my pattern?
What was your favourite thing you did in maths today?
Can you teach me one thing you learned this week?

key_vocabulary.definition: A larger amount — e.g. 5 is more than 3.
A smaller number of something — e.g. 2 is fewer than 5.
Say numbers in order to find out how many there are.
Put objects into groups based on a shared property.
Look at two things to see how they are the same or different.

example_questions: Can you copy this pattern: red, blue, red, blue?
Can you make your own pattern?
content.times_table_tip: At this stage children are not learning times tables formally. Instead they explore equal groups through play — sharing objects fairly or making equal piles — which builds the foundation for multiplication later on.
